key_insight: A geometric multigrid V-cycle can be run entirely on redundant cell-wise vectors, avoiding global assembly while remaining algebraically equivalent to classical constrained multigrid.
engineering_relevance: This simplifies adaptive high-order FEM implementations and enables high-throughput GPU solvers for large-scale elliptic problems with local refinement.
method: Use persistent cell-wise storage so no assembled global vector is formed on any level.
Apply plain tensor-product transfer operators directly to the unassembled residual to reproduce constrained restriction automatically.
Replace explicit edge/interior operator splitting with masked point-Jacobi smoothing on each cell.
evidence_signal: For the Laplace operator, the method shows grid-independent convergence and up to 1.1 GDoF/s per V-cycle on a single GPU in double precision.
watch_item: The approach relies on careful implementation of cell-wise storage and masking; performance and correctness may be sensitive to memory layout and GPU kernel efficiency.
paper_title: Coalesced Matrix-Free Geometric Multigrid on Persistent Cell-Wise Storage
